title: Processes for treating a produced water stream
abstract: A process for separating hydrocarbons from water using supercritical carbon dioxide includes providing water and supercritical carbon dioxide to a mixer, mixing the water and the supercritical carbon dioxide in the mixer, and transferring at least a portion of any hydrocarbons in the water to the supercritical carbon dioxide. In some instances, the mixer can be a static mixer and the mixing can occur in a co-current flow.
claims:
Claims A process for separating hydrocarbons from water using supercritical carbon dioxide, the process comprising at least the steps of: providing a static mixer having at least a first inlet, a second inlet, and an outlet, wherein the static mixer is configured to sustain supercritical carbon dioxide from the first inlet to the outlet; providing a hydrocarbon-rich produced water stream into the first inlet of the static mixer; providing a supercritical carbon dioxide stream into the second inlet of the static mixer; and mixing the hydrocarbon-rich produced water stream and the supercritical carbon dioxide stream to wholly or substantially allow hydrocarbons in the hydrocarbon-rich produced water stream to transfer to the supercritical carbon dioxide stream and produce a mixed hydrocarbon-rich supercritical carbon dioxide and hydrocarbon-lean produced water stream. The process of claim 1, further comprising: passing the mixed hydrocarbon-rich supercritical carbon dioxide and hydrocarbon- lean produced water stream from the outlet of the static mixer into a multiphase separator; separating the mixed hydrocarbon-rich supercritical carbon dioxide and hydrocarbon- lean produced water stream in the separator to produce a separate hydrocarbon-rich supercritical carbon dioxide stream and a separate hydrocarbon-lean produced water stream; and reducing the supercritical conditions of the separate hydrocarbon-rich supercritical carbon dioxide stream to cause a phase change in the carbon dioxide and to produce a gaseous carbon dioxide stream and a recovered hydrocarbon stream. 3. The process of claim 2, further comprising: recovering the gaseous carbon dioxide stream for recycling into the supercritical carbon dioxide stream; and 

 replenishing the supercritical carbon dioxide stream with one or more of a group comprising: a treated exhaust gas, a sweetened amine gas, a permeate gas, a carbon dioxide producing well gas, and a carbon dioxide injection gas. The process of claim 3, further comprising: injecting the hydrocarbon-lean produced water stream into a well wholly or substantially without depressurization. A process for separating hydrocarbons from water using supercritical carbon dioxide, the process comprising: providing water and supercritical carbon dioxide to a mixer, wherein the water comprises hydrocarbons; mixing the water and the supercritical carbon dioxide in the mixer; and transferring at least a portion of the hydrocarbons in the water to the supercritical carbon dioxide. The process of claim 5, wherein the mixer is a static mixer. The process of claim 6, wherein the static mixer has at least a first inlet, a second inlet, and an outlet, and the static mixer is configured to maintain the supercritical carbon dioxide in a supercritical phase from the first inlet to the outlet. The process of claim 5, wherein mixing the water and the supercritical carbon dioxide comprises co-currently mixing the water and the supercritical carbon dioxide. The process of claim 5, wherein providing the water and the supercritical carbon dioxide comprises adding one of the water and the supercritical carbon dioxide to the other upstream of the mixer. The process of claim 5, further comprising: separating the mixed water and supercritical carbon dioxide in a multiphase separator to produce a separate hydrocarbon-rich supercritical carbon dioxide stream and a separate hydrocarbon-lean produced water stream. 1 1. The process of claim 10, further comprising: reducing the supercritical conditions of the separate hydrocarbon-rich supercritical carbon dioxide stream; and causing a phase change in the carbon dioxide to produce a gaseous carbon dioxide stream and a recovered hydrocarbon stream. The process of claim 1 1, further comprising: recovering the carbon dioxide in the gaseous carbon dioxide stream; recycling the recovered carbon dioxide for use as at least a portion of the supercritical carbon dioxide; and replenishing the carbon dioxide in the supercritical carbon dioxide with a treated exhaust gas. 13. The process of claim 10, further comprising: injecting the hydrocarbon-lean produced water stream into a well wholly or substantially without depressurization. 14. The process of claim 5, wherein the water is produced water, and wherein the water is provided as a produced water stream. 15. The process of claim 5, wherein the supercritical carbon dioxide is provided concurrently, or after providing the water. 16. An apparatus to separate hydrocarbons from water, the apparatus comprising: a mixer configured to mix a water stream and a supercritical carbon dioxide stream, wherein the mixer comprises: a first inlet for a supercritical carbon dioxide stream, a second inlet for a hydrocarbon-rich produced water stream, and an outlet, and wherein the mixer is configured to sustain supercritical carbon dioxide from the first inlet to the outlet, to mix the hydrocarbon-rich produced water stream and the supercritical carbon dioxide stream to wholly or substantially allow the hydrocarbons in the hydrocarbon-rich produced water stream to transfer to the supercritical carbon dioxide stream, and to produce a mixed hydrocarbon-rich supercritical carbon dioxide and hydrocarbon-lean produced water stream; a multiphase separator in fluid communication with the mixer, wherein the multiphase separator is configured to: receive the mixed hydrocarbon-rich supercritical carbon dioxide and hydrocarbon-lean produced water stream, separate the mixed hydrocarbon-rich supercritical carbon dioxide and hydrocarbon-lean produced water stream, and produce a separate hydrocarbon-rich supercritical carbon dioxide stream and a separate hydrocarbon-lean produced water stream; and one or more phase changers in fluid communication with the multiphase separator, wherein the one or more phase changers are configured receive the separate hydrocarbon-rich supercritical carbon dioxide stream, to reduce the supercritical conditions of the separate hydrocarbon-rich supercritical carbon dioxide stream, and to cause a phase change in the carbon dioxide to produce a gaseous carbon dioxide stream and a recovered hydrocarbon stream. 17. The apparatus of claim 16, wherein the mixer is a static mixer. 18. The apparatus of claim 16, further comprising: one or more stages or sections for recovery of the one or more supercritical fluids. 19. The apparatus of claim 18, wherein the one or more stages or sections comprise one or more of: C02/hydrocarbon separators, water/C02 separators, valves, phase changers, heat exchangers, compressors, mixers, or a combination of same. 

 The apparatus of claim 16, further comprising: one or more entry valves, wherein the one or more entry valves are configured to change at least one property of a stream prior to the stream entering into a separator. The apparatus of claim 16, further comprising: a valve and water/C02 separator configured to receive the hydrocarbon-lean produced water stream and separate the hydrocarbon-lean produced water stream to produce a carbon dioxide stream and a water stream. The apparatus of claim 16, further comprising: three cascading water/C02 separators configured to increasingly separate the hydrocarbon-lean produced water stream to produce two or more carbon dioxide streams and a water stream. The apparatus of claim 16, further comprising: one or more carbon dioxide stages or sections configured to recycle one or more of the so formed carbon dioxide streams into a supercritical carbon dioxide stream. The apparatus of claim 16, further comprising: one or more carbon dioxide replenishment stages or sections configured to recycle one or more of the so formed carbon dioxide streams into a supercritical carbon dioxide stream.
